visa_types.name: Visiting Friends And Relatives
visa_types.documents_required:
In general the customer is advised to provide documentation in original. Additional documentation may be required. In case the customer cannot give the VAC the original version of the
                                                                        document, a copy must be added and the original shown at the VAC. 
General requirements: 

- Visa application form duly completed, filled in English or Swedish and signed by the customer.
- One recent passport size color
 photograph 
glued to the application.
- Foreign passport issued within the last 10 years, valid at least three months after the scheduled return and contain at least 2 empty pages for the visas. Also all valid passports
                                                                            must be shown in original, as well as previous foreign passports, or if not available, in copy. In case of previous foreign passport, there can be photocopies of previous visas
                                                                            and stamps presented. In case of second valid foreign passport, all copies should be presented.. In case a passport has been annulled or stolen, there has to be a note of it in
                                                                            customer’s domestic passport or a document from the authorities proofing that the passport was stolen.
- Copy 
of the first page of the
 foreign passport 
and copies of all the pages in the foreign passport containing previous visas
- O
riginal
of a
domestic 
passport to be shown at submission and copies of all 16 pages.
- Original and copy of Travel Medical Insurance (TMI) to be provided when applying for the visa covering all Schengen countries with a minimum coverage of 30.000 Euro for: repatriation
                                                                            for medical reasons, urgent medical treatment and emergency admission to hospital treatment during the stay in the territory of the Schengen states. Original of TMI should be
                                                                            shown at the time of documents submission. The insurance must cover 100% of the costs. When applying for single entry visa in addition to the planned duration of stay TMI should
                                                                            have extra 15 days of grace period.When applying for a multiple entry visa, the travel insurance must be valid for the first trip and customer needs to have Travel Medical Insurance
                                                                            for any subsequent visits to the territory of Member States.
- Proof of finances/ maintenance certificate showing sufficient financial means for your upkeep and for the journey home. As per the Swedish authorities you need 450 SEK (or equal in
                                                                            another currency) per day to support yourself during stay in Sweden not including costs for lodging and transportation. Such documents can be e.g. bank statement and activity
                                                                            over the last three months or traveler checks under the name of the customer, duly signed, presented in original and copy. If the person who is inviting you to Sweden will be
                                                                            paying for your support, you must show in the same way that he/she has enough money to do so.
- Proof of professional activity in Ukraine: 
- For employed persons: employment certificate confirming the position of the employee, his/her gained salary within a period of 6 months, the date of employment and the term of vacation
                                                                            AND license/registration of the employer company.
- For unemployed persons (including pensioners, students): proof of sufficient financial means: a document that shows that the host, a family member of the customer or someone else
                                                                            is covering the expenses
- For the self employed person: Certificate of registration of the company in Ukraine, tax declaration for the last 2 quarters, extract from bank account.
- For pensioneers: copy of pension card. Original of pension card should be shown at the time of documents submission.
- For students: certificate from educational institution confirming the type of education (full- time/part-time) and that the customer can return to the study.
- In case of minors 
applying additional documents should be logged along with the application:
- The original of a birth certificate and a copy along with the other documents proving the family ties.
- A written consent from parents/guardians valid for the whole duration of stay in Schengen area and for all Schengen countries: original and a copy, original to be shown while submission.
                                                                            While applying for single entry visa in addition to the planned duration of stay consent should have extra 15 days of grace period.
- In case a child travels alone, there has to be a written consent from both of the parents/guardians.
- If the customer is travelling with one of the parents/guardians, there has to be a written consent from the other.
- Important: 
If one of the parents is travelling together with the child but does not submit his/her visa application along with the child (e.g. already has valid
                                                                            Schengen visa) he/she still needs to submit a legalized written consent. Copies of parent’s passport, either national or international should also be submitted.
- For n
on-Ukrainian citizens 
: a copy of Ukrainian residence permit or a work permit and the original to be shown at submission. Ukrainian residence
                                                                            permit should be valid at least three months (90 days) after the scheduled return from Schengen area.
- Documents proving purpose of travel for visiting family and friends: 

- Invitation (http://www.migrationsverket.se/download/18.5e83388f141c129ba631285c/1460626024243/Inbjudan_241011_eng.pdf)
from the host
                                                                            person stating the host’s contact information and the full name, date of birth and citizenship of the invited person, purpose, time, duration of the visit, number of entries as
                                                                            well as who is responsible for the financial support of the customer in Sweden. It is also required to submit the copy of the inviting person’s residence permit /Passport/ civic
                                                                            registration
- Civic registration/family certificate “personbevis” for the relative in Sweden. Such a document for the purpose of an invitation may be ordered from
Skatteverket, the  Swedish Tax Agency (http://www.skatteverket.se/privat.4.76a43be412206334b89800052864.html)
- And – if the relative/friend will be paying for your support in Sweden – his/her pay slip, work certificate, pension statement or statement of account for the past 6 months.
Important: 
All documents except internal passport must be submitted along with official translation in English or Swedish.
Documents concerning the relative or friend you plan to visit in Sweden must not be older than three months.

                                                                        of the Visa Center Officer. For Urgent Visa eligibility please click here to find Visa Facilitation Agreement between EU and Ukraine (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2OSJO67ytfEvEnDgID43ed/d624ba8e35d67ff7a4cd935a98bfd494/20130701_vfa_agreement_ukraine_en.pdf)                                                                        with effect from 1 July 2013.

Visa Facilitation Agreements between the European Community and the countries of Ukraine, Russia and Moldova, determine the visa fees. For other nationalities the fees are determined
                                                                        by the Visa Code of EU. All paid consular and service fees are non-refundable despite of the decision.

Note: please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/De0cyXtgxcNZZQQzdPKwj/0f2b9d03040d93c4a79a70901569b160/Exemption_visa_fees.pdf) to see the exemptions to the visa fees
visa_types.processing_time:
The normal processing time for Ukrainian nationals is 10 calendar days according to the visa facilitation agreement between the European Community and Ukraine. For the nationals of those
                                                                        countries who do not have a visa facilitation agreement, the processing time is 15 calendar days.
Fast Track: 3 calendar days for Ukrainian Nationals is possible, if the urgency is proven. Also PROVIDED that the supporting documents are not inadequate and the application DOES NOT
                                                                        need more information. 
Please note that you may be required to appear before a Visa Officer for a personal interview at the Embassy of Sweden in Kyiv prior to a decision being taken on your application. Embassy
                                                                        also reserves the right to ask for additional supporting documents during the visa application process. Customers are requested to read the information on this site carefully. The
                                                                        guidelines listed on this site helps you to prepare your documents as accurately as possible. This will reduce the chances of your application being incomplete or delayed

## Photo Specifications
Please note that in case submission of biometrics during application submission at the Visa Centre photo is not mandatory. All customers exempted from the biometric data submission while applying for a visa should submit photograph along with the visa application form.
 ATTENTION! In case if photo is older than 6 months the documents will not be accepted.
A Photograph submitted with the application form should comply with the following specifications:

- Must be an up to date photograph (preferably max 6 months old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 х 4,5 сm
- Face should take 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30-36 mm from point of the chin to head
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat of other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed
Please note that photographs should meet the above mentioned specifications 

## Processing Time
The normal processing time for Ukrainian nationals is 10 calendar days according to the visa facilitation agreement between the European Community and Ukraine. For the nationals of those
                                                                        countries who do not have a visa facilitation agreement, the processing time is 15 calendar days.
Fast Track: 3 calendar days for Ukrainian Nationals is possible, if the urgency is proven. Also PROVIDED that the supporting documents are not inadequate and the application DOES NOT
                                                                        need more information. 
Please note that you may be required to appear before a Visa Officer for a personal interview at the Embassy of Sweden in Kyiv prior to a decision being taken on your application. Embassy
                                                                        also reserves the right to ask for additional supporting documents during the visa application process. Customers are requested to read the information on this site carefully. The
                                                                        guidelines listed on this site helps you to prepare your documents as accurately as possible. This will reduce the chances of your application being incomplete or delayed 

## Download Forms
Forms listed below must be filled in completely (in English or in Swedish languages only) and must be signed by the customer himself/herself.
Please note that application form for Schengen visa  should be signed twice. Application forms with one signature would not be  accepted.
New Visa application form can be downloaded and printed from the link below. Forms are also available at the Visa Application Centre (free of charge). 
Visa Application Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/RnI99aJg2aPEpwVxc2MWv/da34b6ef7a78879f9ad4bee4774fa82a/Schengenvisaapplication.pdf) – in English

OR
- Press the right button on
 your mouse 
on the required link.
- Select
 Save Target As 
from the list
- Save 
the form on your computer.
Note: 
- the application form should be filled in with Latin alphabet
- all mandatory fields should be completed
- print the application form
- do not forget to sign the application

                                                                            must be shown in original, as well as previous foreign passports, or if not available, in copy. In case of previous foreign passport, there can be photocopies of previous visas
                                                                            and stamps presented. In case of second valid foreign passport, all copies should be presented. In case a passport has been annulled or stolen, there has to be a note of it in
                                                                            customer’s domestic passport or a document from the authorities proofing that the passport was stolen.
- Copy 
of the first page of the
 foreign passport 
and copies of all the pages in the foreign passport containing previous visas
- O
riginal
of a
domestic 
passport to be shown at submission and copies of all 16 pages.
- Original and copy of Travel Medical Insurance (TMI) to be provided when applying for the visa covering all Schengen countries with a minimum coverage of 30.000 Euro for: repatriation
                                                                            for medical reasons, urgent medical treatment and emergency admission to hospital treatment during the stay in the territory of the Schengen states. Original of TMI should be
                                                                            shown at the time of documents submission. The insurance must cover 100% of the costs. When applying for single entry visa in addition to the planned duration of stay TMI should
                                                                            have extra 15 days of grace period.When applying for a multiple entry visa, the travel insurance must be valid for the first trip and customer needs to have Travel Medical Insurance
                                                                            for any subsequent visits to the territory of Member States.
- Proof of finances/ maintenance certificate showing sufficient financial means for your upkeep and for the journey home. As per the Swedish authorities you need 450 SEK (or equal in
                                                                            another currency) per day to support yourself during stay in Sweden not including costs for lodging and transportation. Such documents can be e.g. bank statement and activity
                                                                            over the last three months or traveler checks under the name of the customer, duly signed, presented in original and copy. If the person who is inviting you to Sweden will be
                                                                            paying for your support, you must show in the same way that he/she has enough money to do so.
- Proof of professional activity in Ukraine: 
- For employed persons: employment certificate confirming the position of the employee, his/her gained salary within a period of 6 months, the date of employment and the term of vacation
                                                                            AND license/registration of the employer company.
- For unemployed persons (including pensioners, students): proof of sufficient financial means: a document that shows that the host, a family member of the customer or someone else
                                                                            is covering the expenses
- For the self employed person: Certificate of registration of the company in Ukraine, tax declaration for the last 2 quarters, extract from bank account.
- For pensioneers: copy of pension card. Original of pension card should be shown at the time of documents submission.
- For students: certificate from educational institution confirming the type of education (full- time/part-time) and that the customer can return to the study.
10. In case of minors 
applying additional documents should be logged along with the application:
- The original of a birth certificate and a copy along with the other documents proving the family ties.
- A written consent from parents/guardians valid for the whole duration of stay in Schengen area and for all Schengen countries: original and a copy, original to be shown while submission.
                                                                            While applying for single entry visa in addition to the planned duration of stay consent should have extra 15 days of grace period.
- In case a child travels alone, there has to be a written consent from both of the parents/guardians.
- If the customer is travelling with one of the parents/guardians, there has to be a written consent from the other.

If one of the parents is travelling together with the child but does not submit his/her visa application along with the child (e.g. already has valid
                                                                            Schengen visa) he/she still needs to submit a legalized written consent. Copies of parent’s passport, either national or international should also be submitted.

on-Ukrainian citizens 
: a copy of Ukrainian residence permit or a work permit and the original to be shown at submission. Ukrainian residence
                                                                            permit should be valid at least three months (90 days) after the scheduled return from Schengen area.
12. Documents proving purpose of travel as a lorry driver: 

- A written invitation either from “Association of International Road Carriers” (AsMAP), State Enterprise “Service on International Road Carriages” (SE “SIRC”),
                                                                            “Ukrainian Road Transport and Logistics Union”, or from “All-Ukrainian Association of Automobile carriers” (AAAC). In the invitation it should be stated
                                                                            the purpose of the trips, the length and the frequency.

- Lorry Drivers employed by companies that are not the members of any of the above mentioned organisations also need to submit the Invitation from the Swedish Company, for which the
                                                                            transports should be made. A copy of work book and drivers license for the lorry driver in original with copies also required.

- Introduction letter from your employer including your position, how long you have been working for the company, what your salary is and purpose of the visit. The letter should contain
                                                                            contact information to the company, the name of the person signing as well as his/her signature. If you are a private entrepreneur you should submit the registration certificate
                                                                            for the company.

- Original CMR with Photocopy, License and List of Workbook.

Important: all documents except internal passport must be submitted along with official translation in English or Swedish.

## Photo Specifications
Please note that in case submission of biometrics during application submission at the Visa Centre photo is not mandatory. All customers exempted from the biometric data submission while applying for a visa should submit photograph along with the visa application form.
ATTENTION! In case if photo is older than 6 months the documents will not be accepted.
A Photograph submitted with the application form should comply with the following specifications:

- Must be an up to date photograph (preferably max 6 months old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 х 4,5 сm
- Face should take 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30-36 mm from point of the chin to head
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat of other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed
Please note that photographs should meet the above mentioned specifications 

## Processing Time
The normal processing time for Ukrainian nationals is 10 calendar days according to the visa facilitation agreement between the European Community and Ukraine. For the nationals of those
                                                                        countries who do not have a visa facilitation agreement, the processing time is 15 calendar days.
Fast Track: 3 calendar days for Ukrainian Nationals is possible, if the urgency is proven. Also PROVIDED that the supporting documents are not inadequate and the application DOES NOT
                                                                        need more information. 
Please note that you may be required to appear before a Visa Officer for a personal interview at the Embassy of Sweden in Kyiv prior to a decision being taken on your application. Embassy
                                                                        also reserves the right to ask for additional supporting documents during the visa application process. Customers are requested to read the information on this site carefully. The
                                                                        guidelines listed on this site helps you to prepare your documents as accurately as possible. This will reduce the chances of your application being incomplete or delayed 

## Download Forms
Forms listed below must be filled in completely (in English or in Swedish languages only) and must be signed by the customer himself/herself.
Please note that application form for Schengen visa  should be signed twice. Application forms with one signature would not be  accepted.

 Save Target As

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This category of visa caters for people wishing to stay in Sweden for a period maximum 3 months to visit the member of his/her family.
Customer will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category. 

## Visa Fees

[Table:Close Family Members]
* payable cash in Hryvnia according to the monthly course rate defined by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Sweden

** Urgent Visa will be processed during three (3) working days or less. If you have a reason to believe that your application should be processed urgently, please bring it to the notice
                                                                        of the Visa Center Officer. For Urgent Visa eligibility please click here to find Visa Facilitation Agreement between EU and Ukraine (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2OSJO67ytfEvEnDgID43ed/d624ba8e35d67ff7a4cd935a98bfd494/20130701_vfa_agreement_ukraine_en.pdf)                                                                        with effect from 1 July 2013. 

*** payable cash in local currency (Hryvnia) per customer . Fee is inclusive of 20% VAT

Visa Facilitation Agreements between the European Community and the countries of Ukraine, Russia and Moldova, determine the visa fees. For other nationalities the fees are determined
                                                                        by the Visa Code of EU. All paid consular and service fees are non-refundable despite of the decision.

Note: please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/De0cyXtgxcNZZQQzdPKwj/0f2b9d03040d93c4a79a70901569b160/Exemption_visa_fees.pdf) to see the exemptions to the visa fees 

## Documents Required
In general the customer is advised to provide documentation in original. Additional documentation may be required. In case the customer cannot give the VAC the original version of the
                                                                        document, a copy must be added and the original shown at the VAC. 
General requirements: 

- Visa application form duly completed, filled in English or Swedish and signed by the customer.
- One recent passport size color
  photograph 
glued to the application.
- Foreign passport issued within the last 10 years, valid at least three months after the scheduled return and contain at least 2 empty pages for the visas. Also all valid passports
                                                                            must be shown in original, as well as previous foreign passports, or if not available, in copy. In case of previous foreign passport, there can be photocopies of previous visas
                                                                            and stamps presented. In case of second valid foreign passport, all copies should be presented.. In case a passport has been annulled or stolen, there has to be a note of it in
                                                                            customer’s domestic passport or a document from the authorities proofing that the passport was stolen.
- Copy 
of the first page of the
 foreign passport 
and copies of all the pages in the foreign passport containing previous visas
- O
riginal
of a
domestic 
passport to be shown at submission and copies of all 16 pages.
- Original and copy of Travel Medical Insurance (TMI) to be provided when applying for the visa covering all Schengen countries with a minimum coverage of 30.000 Euro for: repatriation
                                                                            for medical reasons, urgent medical treatment and emergency admission to hospital treatment during the stay in the territory of the Schengen states. Original of TMI should be
                                                                            shown at the time of documents submission. The insurance must cover 100% of the costs. When applying for single entry visa in addition to the planned duration of stay TMI should
                                                                            have extra 15 days of grace period.When applying for a multiple entry visa, the travel insurance must be valid for the first trip and customer needs to have Travel Medical Insurance
                                                                            for any subsequent visits to the territory of Member States.
- Proof of finances/ maintenance certificate showing sufficient financial means for your upkeep and for the journey home. As per the Swedish authorities you need 450 SEK (or equal in
                                                                            another currency) per day to support yourself during stay in Sweden not including costs for lodging and transportation. Such documents can be e.g. bank statement and activity
                                                                            over the last three months or traveler checks under the name of the customer, duly signed, presented in original and copy. If the person who is inviting you to Sweden will be
                                                                            paying for your support, you must show in the same way that he/she has enough money to do so.
- Proof of professional activity in Ukraine: 
- For employed persons: employment certificate confirming the position of the employee, his/her gained salary within a period of 6 months, the date of employment and the term of vacation
                                                                            AND license/registration of the employer company.
- For unemployed persons (including pensioners, students): proof of sufficient financial means: a document that shows that the host, a family member of the customer or someone else
                                                                            is covering the expenses
- For the self employed person: Certificate of registration of the company in Ukraine, tax declaration for the last 2 quarters, extract from bank account.
- For pensioneers: copy of pension card. Original of pension card should be shown at the time of documents submission.
- For students: certificate from educational institution confirming the type of education (full- time/part-time) and that the customer can return to the study.
- In case of minors 
applying additional documents should be logged along with the application:
- The original of a birth certificate and a copy along with the other documents proving the family ties.
- A written consent from parents/guardians valid for the whole duration of stay in Schengen area and for all Schengen countries: original and a copy, original to be shown while submission.
                                                                            While applying for single entry visa in addition to the planned duration of stay consent should have extra 15 days of grace period.
- In case a child travels alone, there has to be a written consent from both of the parents/guardians.
- If the customer is travelling with one of the parents/guardians, there has to be a written consent from the other.
- Important: 
If one of the parents is travelling together with the child but does not submit his/her visa application along with the child (e.g. already has valid
                                                                            Schengen visa) he/she still needs to submit a legalized written consent. Copies of parent’s passport, either national or international should also be submitted.
- For n
on-Ukrainian citizens 
: a copy of Ukrainian residence permit or a work permit and the original to be shown at submission. Ukrainian residence
                                                                            permit should be valid at least three months (90 days) after the scheduled return from Schengen area.
- Documents proving purpose of travel for visiting family and friends: 

- Invitation (http://www.migrationsverket.se/download/18.5e83388f141c129ba631285c/1460626024243/Inbjudan_241011_eng.pdf)
from the host
                                                                            person stating the host’s contact information and the full name, date of birth and citizenship of the invited person, purpose, time, duration of the visit, number of entries as
                                                                            well as who is responsible for the financial support of the customer in Sweden. It is also required to submit the copy of the inviting person’s residence permit /Passport/ civic
                                                                            registration
- Civic registration/family certificate “personbevis” for the relative in Sweden. Such a document for the purpose of an invitation may be ordered from
Skatteverket, the  Swedish Tax Agency (http://www.skatteverket.se/privat.4.76a43be412206334b89800052864.html)
- And – if the relative/friend will be paying for your support in Sweden – his/her pay slip, work certificate, pension statement or statement of account for the past 6 months.
Important: 
All documents except internal passport must be submitted along with official translation in English or Swedish.

                                                                        Documents concerning the relative or friend you plan to visit in Sweden must not be older than three months. If close family ties exists to a person in Sweden having been granted a permanent residence permit or is a Swedish citizen, it is possible to
                                                                        apply for a visa valid for up to 5 years. Close family ties can be: spouse, children, parents, siblings, grandchildren and grandparents.
In case of travelling to siblings, customer can submit documents on the visa category “Visiting Friends and Relatives”.

## Photo Specifications
Please note that in case submission of biometrics during application submission at the Visa Centre photo is not mandatory. All customers exempted from the biometric data submission while applying for a visa should submit photograph along with the visa application form.

 ATTENTION! In case if photo is older than 6 months the documents will not be accepted.
A Photograph submitted with the application form should comply with the following specifications:

- Must be an up to date photograph (preferably max 6 months old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 х 4,5 сm
- Face should take 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30-36 mm from point of the chin to head
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat of other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed
Please note that photographs should meet the above mentioned specifications 

## Processing Time
The normal processing time for Ukrainian nationals is 10 calendar days according to the visa facilitation agreement between the European Community and Ukraine. For the nationals of those
                                                                        countries who do not have a visa facilitation agreement, the processing time is 15 calendar days.
Fast Track: 3 calendar days for Ukrainian Nationals is possible, if the urgency is proven. Also PROVIDED that the supporting documents are not inadequate and the application DOES NOT
                                                                        need more information. 
Please note that you may be required to appear before a Visa Officer for a personal interview at the Embassy of Sweden in Kyiv prior to a decision being taken on your application. Embassy
                                                                        also reserves the right to ask for additional supporting documents during the visa application process. Customers are requested to read the information on this site carefully. The
                                                                        guidelines listed on this site helps you to prepare your documents as accurately as possible. This will reduce the chances of your application being incomplete or delayed 

## Download Forms
Forms listed below must be filled in completely (in English or in Swedish languages only) and must be signed by the customer himself/herself.
Please note that application form for Schengen visa  should be signed twice. Application forms with one signature would not be  accepted.
New Visa application form can be downloaded and printed from the link below. Forms are also available at the Visa Application Centre (free of charge). 
Visa Application Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/RnI99aJg2aPEpwVxc2MWv/da34b6ef7a78879f9ad4bee4774fa82a/Schengenvisaapplication.pdf) – in English 
To Download a Form:
- Click on the
 form 
link
- When the form opens, save the form on
 your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on
 your mouse 
on the required link.
- Select
 Save Target As 
from the list
- Save 
the form on your computer.
Note: 
- the application form should be filled in with Latin alphabet
- all mandatory fields should be completed
- print the application form
- do not forget to sign the application

                                                                            about who covers the costs should be indicated. A list of participants should be enclosed stating the full name, date of birth, citizenship and function of each participant.

Important: all documents except internal passport must be submitted along with official translation in English or Swedish.

## Photo Specifications
Please note that in case submission of biometrics during application submission at the Visa Centre photo is not mandatory. All customers exempted from the biometric data submission while applying for a visa should submit photograph along with the visa application form.
ATTENTION! In case if photo is older than 6 months the documents will not be accepted.
A Photograph submitted with the application form should comply with the following specifications:

- Must be an up to date photograph (preferably max 6 months old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 х 4,5 сm
- Face should take 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30-36 mm from point of the chin to head
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat of other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed
Please note that photographs should meet the above mentioned specifications

The normal processing time for Ukrainian nationals is 10 calendar days according to the visa facilitation agreement between the European Community and Ukraine. For the nationals of those
                                                                        countries who do not have a visa facilitation agreement, the processing time is 15 calendar days.
Fast Track: 3 calendar days for Ukrainian Nationals is possible, if the urgency is proven. Also PROVIDED that the supporting documents are not inadequate and the application DOES NOT
                                                                        need more information. 
Please note that you may be required to appear before a Visa Officer for a personal interview at the Embassy of Sweden in Kyiv prior to a decision being taken on your application. Embassy
                                                                        also reserves the right to ask for additional supporting documents during the visa application process. Customers are requested to read the information on this site carefully. The
                                                                        guidelines listed on this site helps you to prepare your documents as accurately as possible. This will reduce the chances of your application being incomplete or delayed

- Visa application form 
duly completed, filled in English or Swedish and signed by the customer.
- One recent passport size color
 photograph 
glued to the application.
- Foreign passport issued within the last 10 years, valid at least three months after the scheduled return and contain at least 2 empty pages for the visas. Also all valid passports
                                                                            must be shown in original, as well as previous foreign passports, or if not available, in copy. In case of previous foreign passport, there can be photocopies of previous visas
                                                                            and stamps presented. In case of second valid foreign passport, all copies should be presented. In case a passport has been annulled or stolen, there has to be a note of it in
                                                                            customer’s domestic passport or a document from the authorities proofing that the passport was stolen.
- Copy 
of the first page of the
 foreign passport 
and copies of all the pages in the foreign passport containing previous visas
- O
riginal
of a
domestic 
passport to be shown at submission and copies of all 16 pages.
- Original and copy of Travel Medical Insurance (TMI) to be provided when applying for the visa covering all Schengen countries with a minimum coverage of 30.000 Euro for: repatriation
                                                                            for medical reasons, urgent medical treatment and emergency admission to hospital treatment during the stay in the territory of the Schengen states. Original of TMI should be
                                                                            shown at the time of documents submission. The insurance must cover 100% of the costs. When applying for single entry visa in addition to the planned duration of stay TMI should
                                                                            have extra 15 days of grace period.When applying for a multiple entry visa, the travel insurance must be valid for the first trip and customer needs to have Travel Medical Insurance
                                                                            for any subsequent visits to the territory of Member States.
- Proof of finances/ maintenance certificate showing sufficient financial means for your upkeep and for the journey home. As per the Swedish authorities you need 450 SEK (or equal in
                                                                            another currency) per day to support yourself during stay in Sweden not including costs for lodging and transportation. Such documents can be e.g. bank statement and activity
                                                                            over the last three months or traveler checks under the name of the customer, duly signed, presented in original and copy. If the person who is inviting you to Sweden will be
                                                                            paying for your support, you must show in the same way that he/she has enough money to do so.
- Proof of professional activity in Ukraine: 
- For employed persons: employment certificate confirming the position of the employee, his/her gained salary within a period of 6 months, the date of employment and the term of vacation
                                                                            AND license/registration of the employer company.
- For unemployed persons (including pensioners, students): proof of sufficient financial means: a document that shows that the host, a family member of the customer or someone else
                                                                            is covering the expenses
- For the self employed person: Certificate of registration of the company in Ukraine, tax declaration for the last 2 quarters, extract from bank account.
- For pensioneers: copy of pension card. Original of pension card should be shown at the time of documents submission.
- For students: certificate from educational institution confirming the type of education (full- time/part-time) and that the customer can return to the study.
9. In case of minors 
applying additional documents should be logged along with the application:
- The original of a birth certificate and a copy along with the other documents proving the family ties.
- A written consent from parents/guardians valid for the whole duration of stay in Schengen area and for all Schengen countries: original and a copy, original to be shown while submission.
                                                                            While applying for single entry visa in addition to the planned duration of stay consent should have extra 15 days of grace period.
- In case a child travels alone, there has to be a written consent from both of the parents/guardians.
- If the customer is travelling with one of the parents/guardians, there has to be a written consent from the other.
- Important:
If one of the parents is travelling together with the child but does not submit his/her visa application along with the child (e.g. already has valid Schengen
                                                                            visa) he/she still needs to submit a legalized written consent. Copies of parent’s passport, either national or international should also be submitted.
10. For n
on-Ukrainian citizens 
: a copy of Ukrainian residence permit or a work permit and the original to be shown at submission. Ukrainian residence
                                                                            permit should be valid at least three months (90 days) after the scheduled return from Schengen area.
11. Documents proving purpose of travel for a business visit: 

- A written invitation from a host legal person or company, or an office or branch of such legal person or company, state and local authorities of Sweden or organizing committees of
                                                                            trade and industrial exhibitions, conferences and symposia held in Sweden. The invitation should contain data of the host company, full name, date of birth and nationality of
                                                                            the invited person, purpose, date, duration of visit, number of scheduled trips, as well as who is responsible for the financial support of the customer in Sweden. The invitation
                                                                            should be signed by the inviting person and include the seal of the host company or organization. It is possible to apply with a scanned copy of this invitation.
Cultural visit: 
- An invitation from the host organization organising the event in Sweden, showing both the duration and purpose of the visit and who will be responsible for supporting you financially
                                                                            during your stay.
Study visit: 
- If the purpose is study visit for pupils/students – a written request or a certificate of enrolment from the host university, college or school or student cards or certificates of
                                                                            the courses to be attended.
Important: all documents except internal passport must be submitted along with official translation in English or Swedish.

## Overview
This category does not cover freelance journalists.
Customer will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category. 

## Visa Fees

[Table:Journalists]
* payable cash in Hryvnia according to the monthly course rate defined by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Sweden

** Urgent Visa will be processed during three (3) working days or less. If you have a reason to believe that your application should be processed urgently, please bring it to the notice
                                                                        of the Visa Center Officer. For Urgent Visa eligibility please click here to find Visa Facilitation Agreement between EU and Ukraine (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2OSJO67ytfEvEnDgID43ed/d624ba8e35d67ff7a4cd935a98bfd494/20130701_vfa_agreement_ukraine_en.pdf)                                                                        with effect from 1 July 2013. 

*** payable cash in local currency (Hryvnia) per customer . Fee is inclusive of 20% VAT

Visa Facilitation Agreements between the European Community and the countries of Ukraine, Russia and Moldova, determine the visa fees. For other nationalities the fees are determined
                                                                        by the Visa Code of EU. All paid consular and service fees are non-refundable despite of the decision.

Note: please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/De0cyXtgxcNZZQQzdPKwj/0f2b9d03040d93c4a79a70901569b160/Exemption_visa_fees.pdf) to see the exemptions to the visa fees 

## Documents Required
In general the customer is advised to provide documentation in original. Additional documentation may be required. In case the customer cannot give the VAC the original version of the
                                                                        document, a copy must be added and the original shown at the VAC.
General requirements: 

- Visa application form duly completed, filled in English or Swedish and signed by the customer.
- One recent passport size color
 photograph 
glued to the application.
- Foreign passport issued within the last 10 years, valid at least three months after the scheduled return and contain at least 2 empty pages for the visas. Also all valid passports
                                                                            must be shown in original, as well as previous foreign passports, or if not available, in copy. In case of previous foreign passport, there can be photocopies of previous visas
                                                                            and stamps presented. In case of second valid foreign passport, all copies should be presented.. In case a passport has been annulled or stolen, there has to be a note of it in
                                                                            customer’s domestic passport or a document from the authorities proofing that the passport was stolen.
- Copy 
of the first page of the
 foreign passport 
and copies of all the pages in the foreign passport containing previous visas
- O
riginal
of a
domestic 
passport to be shown at submission and copies of all 16 pages.
- Original and copy of Travel Medical Insurance (TMI) to be provided when applying for the visa covering all Schengen countries with a minimum coverage of 30.000 Euro for: repatriation
                                                                            for medical reasons, urgent medical treatment and emergency admission to hospital treatment during the stay in the territory of the Schengen states. Original of TMI should be
                                                                            shown at the time of documents submission. The insurance must cover 100% of the costs. When applying for single entry visa in addition to the planned duration of stay TMI should
                                                                            have extra 15 days of grace period.When applying for a multiple entry visa, the travel insurance must be valid for the first trip and customer needs to have Travel Medical Insurance
                                                                            for any subsequent visits to the territory of Member States.
- Proof of finances/ maintenance certificate showing sufficient financial means for your upkeep and for the journey home. As per the Swedish authorities you need 450 SEK (or equal in
                                                                            another currency) per day to support yourself during stay in Sweden not including costs for lodging and transportation. Such documents can be e.g. bank statement and activity
                                                                            over the last three months or traveler checks under the name of the customer, duly signed, presented in original and copy. If the person who is inviting you to Sweden will be
                                                                            paying for your support, you must show in the same way that he/she has enough money to do so.
- Proof of professional activity in Ukraine: 
- For employed persons: employment certificate confirming the position of the employee, his/her gained salary within a period of 6 months, the date of employment and the term of vacation
                                                                            AND license/registration of the employer company.
- For unemployed persons (including pensioners, students): proof of sufficient financial means: a document that shows that the host, a family member of the customer or someone else
                                                                            is covering the expenses
- For the self employed person: Certificate of registration of the company in Ukraine, tax declaration for the last 2 quarters, extract from bank account.
- For pensioneers: copy of pension card. Original of pension card should be shown at the time of documents submission.
- For students: certificate from educational institution confirming the type of education (full- time/part-time) and that the customer can return to the study.
9. In case of minors
applying additional documents should be logged along with the application:
- The original of a birth certificate and a copy along with the other documents proving the family ties.
- A written consent from parents/guardians valid for the whole duration of stay in Schengen area and for all Schengen countries: original and a copy, original to be shown while submission.
                                                                            While applying for single entry visa in addition to the planned duration of stay consent should have extra 15 days of grace period.
- In case a child travels alone, there has to be a written consent from both of the parents/guardians.
- If the customer is travelling with one of the parents/guardians, there has to be a written consent from the other.
- Important:
If one of the parents is travelling together with the child but does not submit his/her visa application along with the child (e.g. already has valid
                                                                            Schengen visa) he/she still needs to submit a legalized written consent. Copies of parent’s passport, either national or international should also be submitted.
10. For n
on-Ukrainian citizens 
: a copy of Ukrainian residence permit or a work permit and the original to be shown at submission. Ukrainian residence
                                                                            permit should be valid at least three months (90 days) after the scheduled return from Schengen area.
Documents proving purpose of  travel as journalists or a member of a technical crew accompanying them:

- A copy of a certificate or other document issued by the competent Ukrainian professional organization proving that the person concerned is a qualified (professional) journalist, for
                                                                            ex. national professional journalist’s card. The original to be shown at submission. A certificate or another document issued by either “National Union of Journalists of Ukraine”
                                                                            (NUJU), “Independent Media Union of Ukraine” (IMUU), “Kyiv Independent Media Union” (KIMU), “Academy of Ukrainian Press” (AUP), or “Regional Press Development Institute” (RPDI),
                                                                            that proves that the customer is a qualified journalist travelling to Sweden to carry our journalistic work
- Proof that the person is a member of the technical crew accompanying the journalist in a professional capacity.
Important: all documents except internal passport must be submitted along with official translation in English or Swedish.

## Photo Specifications
Please note that in case submission of biometrics during application submission at the Visa Centre photo is not mandatory. All customers exempted from the biometric data submission while applying for a visa should submit photograph along with the visa application form.

ATTENTION! In case if photo is older than 6 months the documents will not be accepted.
A Photograph submitted with the application form should comply with the following specifications:

- Must be an up to date photograph (preferably max 6 months old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 х 4,5 сm
- Face should take 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30-36 mm from point of the chin to head
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat of other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed
Please note that photographs should meet the above mentioned specifications 

## Processing Time
The normal processing time for Ukrainian nationals is 10 calendar days according to the visa facilitation agreement between the European Community and Ukraine. For the nationals of those
                                                                        countries who do not have a visa facilitation agreement, the processing time is 15 calendar days.
Fast Track: 3 calendar days for Ukrainian Nationals is possible, if the urgency is proven. Also PROVIDED that the supporting documents are not inadequate and the application DOES NOT
                                                                        need more information. 
Please note that you may be required to appear before a Visa Officer for a personal interview at the Embassy of Sweden in Kyiv prior to a decision being taken on your application. Embassy
                                                                        also reserves the right to ask for additional supporting documents during the visa application process. Customers are requested to read the information on this site carefully. The
                                                                        guidelines listed on this site helps you to prepare your documents as accurately as possible. This will reduce the chances of your application being incomplete or delayed 

## Download Forms
Forms listed below must be filled in completely (in English or in Swedish languages only) and must be signed by the customer himself/herself.
Please note that application form for Schengen visa  should be signed twice. Application forms with one signature would not be  accepted.
New Visa application form can be downloaded and printed from the link below. Forms are also available at the Visa Application Centre (free of charge). 
Visa Application Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/RnI99aJg2aPEpwVxc2MWv/da34b6ef7a78879f9ad4bee4774fa82a/Schengenvisaapplication.pdf) – in English
